subject: DevOps
title: DevOps at Etsy
description: A short lesson to help you apply DevOps at Etsy.

hook.text: Etsy went from deploying twice a week to 50 times a day. They became the poster child of DevOps culture.

buildup.text: They built Deployinator — a one-button deploy tool any engineer could use. No gatekeepers.

discovery.text: Feature flags let them deploy code without activating it. Turn features on for 1% of users first.

twist.text: Etsy published blameless postmortems publicly. Failures became learning tools for the whole industry.

climax.text: Their philosophy: if it hurts, do it more often. Deploys hurt, so deploy 50 times a day until it's boring.

punchline.text: Etsy made deployment boring. That's the highest compliment.

quiz.question: What was Etsy's approach to painful deployments?
options: Do it more often until it becomes routine
Reduce deployment frequency to once a month
